official_functions.role: Construct the official Play clientbound remove_entities fixture
Write, decode, and observe the official remove_entities body
Name the packet id assigned in Play clientbound state

invariants: The official Play clientbound table assigns remove_entities to packet id 0x4d.
The official Play clientbound protocol codec dispatches the remove_entities id to ClientboundRemoveEntitiesPacket.
The official remove_entities body is an entity id list encoded by FriendlyByteBuf.writeIntIdList.
The fixture uses primitive entity ids and does not require initialized Entity or Level state.

The framed bytes, packet table id, body bytes, decoded packet class, decoded entity ids, Play clientbound packet table, and remaining decode byte counts are generated by the official jar-backed answer artifact.
This contract does not prove entity existence, world state, render readiness, or client-load completion.
test_topology_note: rust_test_target names the current case adapter only; the manifest owns the runnable test topology.
stop_boundary: Do not use this framed dispatch/decode contract to infer entity existence, world state, render readiness, or client-load completion.
